Dictionary.Phrase: DB Import Plus - Device {0} Properties Version {1}
Log the result of a query from the database (debugging)

On the Database tab, you need to specify the database type and specify the parameters of the database connection. If you need to enter non-standard connection parameters, you can edit the connection string directly. Examples of connection parameters can be found on the website https://www.connectionstrings.com .
The driver assumes two formats for receiving data - these are "Tags based on the list of requested table columns" and "Tags based on the list of requested table rows". In the format "KP tags based on the list of requested table columns", the SQL query is compiled so that in the column name there will be the name of the KP tag, and in the first line the value.
Example, given the table
+----------+----------+----------+ | COLUMN_1 | COLUMN_2 | COLUMN_3 | +----------+----------+----------+ | 1,000 | 2,000 | 3,000 | +----------+----------+----------+ | 3,000 | 4,000 | 5,000 | +----------+----------+----------+ | 6,000 | 7,000 | 8,000 | +----------+----------+----------+
If each column in the data source is the value of one variable, then the query will be simple: SELECT COLUMN_1 AS TAGNAME_1, COLUMN_2 AS TAGNAME_2, COLUMN_3 AS TAGNAME_3 FROM DATATABLE_NAME WHERE CONDITION
If the data source in this example, +----------+--------------+----------+ | COLUMN_1 | COLUMN_2 | COLUMN_3 | +----------+--------------+----------+ | 1 | TAGNAME_1 | 1,000 | +----------+--------------+----------+ | 2 | TAGNAME_2 | 2,000 | +----------+--------------+----------+ | 3 | TAGNAME_3 | 3,000 | +----------+--------------+----------+
then the easiest way to flip would be the following query: SELECT MAX(CASE WHEN [COLUMN_2] ='TAGNAME_1' THEN COLUMN_3 ELSE NULL END) AS 'TAGNAME_1', MAX(CASE WHEN [COLUMN_2] ='TAGNAME_2' THEN COLUMN_3 ELSE NULL END) AS 'TAGNAME_2', MAX(CASE WHEN [COLUMN_2] ='TAGNAME_3' THEN COLUMN_3 ELSE NULL END) AS 'TAGNAME_3' FROM DATATABLE_NAME WHERE CONDITION
MS SQL. In connection with the transition to the Microsoft.Data.SqlClient library pay attention to using the connection for remote work! You may need to add a condition in the Additional Parameters field in the connection string Stored security information=True;Trusted Server Certificate=True;
MySQL. MySQL has a feature in SSL mode. For it, you need to specify whether to use SSL or not. There are several options, but specify only one. They are equivalent in value. You only need to use one word. 1) None or Disabled 2) Preferred or Prefered 3) Required 4) VerifyCA 5) VerifyFull
Commands. The commands are defined in the configuration.
In version 6.3.0.1, it is now possible to write string values command. If a command is created, a command tag with a length of 20 tags will be automatically generated in the communicator driver. This size assumes that 80 characters will be enough for any string value. It is forbidden to create other tags at these addresses! There will be data distortion!
In version 6.3.0.2, commands can now specify the length in characters.
Command example: INSERT INTO DATATABLE_NAME (COLUMN_1, COLUMN_2, COLUMN_3) VALUES ('TAGNAME00001', @cmdVal, GETDATE());
UPDATE DATATABLE_NAME SET COLUMN_2 = @cmdVal WHERE COLUMN_1 = @cmdNum;
Format of Tags from the Database
Maximum number of characters in a string command
Tags based on the list of requested table columns
Tags based on the list of requested table rows
Format of Tags from the Database
Maximum number of characters in a string command
Tags based on the list of requested table columns
Tags based on the list of requested table rows
Maximum number of characters in word
